Badri Patel

MA Psychology Candidate & Neuropsychology Researcher

Pandit Deendayal Energy University (PDEU), Gandhinagar, India

My research centres on the neuropsychology of memory, attention, and learning disabilities, with a focus on how structured, holistic intervention models support cognitive rehabilitation in children with developmental delay. I bridge clinical case-based intervention design with quantitative outcome measurement.

Publications Research Contact
About

Researcher & Practitioner

Bridging clinical neuropsychology with rigorous quantitative research to advance cognitive rehabilitation.

I am currently pursuing a Master of Arts in Psychology at Pandit Deendayal Energy University (PDEU), Gandhinagar, following a Bachelor of Arts in Psychology from Ahmedabad University. My academic journey has been shaped by a deep commitment to understanding the neuropsychological underpinnings of developmental disorders and designing evidence-based interventions.

Over the past three years, I have worked as a Student Intern at Healvibe Clinic in Ahmedabad, conducting neuropsychological assessments, behavioural observations, and developmental evaluations for paediatric clients. This clinical experience has directly informed my research, allowing me to ground theoretical models in real-world practice.

My future aspiration is to pursue doctoral research that extends my work on the Integrated Neuropsychological Learning Model (INLM) to broader populations, exploring emotional regulation and neuroplasticity across the lifespan.

Research Philosophy

"Every child deserves a research-backed path to cognitive growth. I believe in translating neuroscientific insight into compassionate, structured, and measurable intervention — where clinical rigour meets human warmth."
Research

Research Interests

Investigating the intersection of neuropsychology, cognitive rehabilitation, and developmental science.

🧠

Memory & Attention

Examining parietal, sensorimotor, and thalamocortical contributions to memory, attention, and motor function in children with developmental delays.

🔬

INLM Framework

Developing and validating the Integrated Neuropsychological Learning Model for cognitive rehabilitation in intellectual disability and specific learning difficulties.

⚡

Neuroplasticity

Investigating neuroplasticity and executive-motor integration through structured interventions like hopscotch-based motor training and shaping protocols.

🎯

Dopamine & Working Memory

Exploring relationships between dopaminergic function, frontal-temporal lobe activity, and working memory, attention, and reaction time.

📊

Behavioural Finance

Analysing investor decision-making under uncertainty using SPSS and ANOVA, bridging psychology with economic behaviour.

🌱

Sustainability Psychology

Studying consumer behaviour change through financial incentives, social proof, and loss-aversion framing on reusable-cup adoption.

Projects

Research Projects

Ongoing and completed research initiatives spanning clinical neuropsychology, behavioural science, and sustainability.

Project Self — Dopamine, Working Memory, and Self-Regulation

Investigating relationships between dopaminergic function, frontal-temporal lobe activity, and working memory, attention, and reaction time. Developing an applied framework on habit formation, self-discipline, and self-improvement grounded in a neurocognitive model.

Dec 2024 – PresentManuscript in preparation

Clinical Case Studies — Integrated Neuropsychological Learning Model (INLM)

Co-authored a series of single-case neuropsychological studies applying the INLM framework to developmental delay, intellectual disability, and specific learning difficulties. Designed structured interventions including hopscotch-based motor training and shaping protocols for independent feeding.

2024 – Present12+ PublicationsCase Study Methodology

Learned Helplessness and Cognitive Distortions

Surveyed 500+ individuals to examine behavioural responses to uncontrollable negative outcomes. Analysing associations between learned helplessness and cognitive distortion patterns.

Apr 2023 – Present500+ ParticipantsManuscript in preparation

"Carry Your Own Cup" Sustainability Campaign

Designed and led a mixed-methods study on consumer behaviour change, surveying 500 participants to evaluate the effect of financial incentives, social proof, and loss-aversion framing on reusable-cup adoption. Translated findings into a marketing strategy piloted with local cafés.

Sep 2024 – May 2025Ahmedabad University CapstoneMixed-Methods
